patterns.name: Identical client ShutDown() lifecycle boilerplate across cache clients
patterns.rationale: ObjectClient::ShutDown, KVClient::ShutDown and StreamClient::ShutDown have byte-identical bodies (trace guard, impl_ null-check, impl_->ShutDown/CompleteHandler, return). This lifecycle teardown logic is copy-pasted verbatim into every client; a change to shutdown/rollback semantics must be edited in three (and growing) places, and divergence risks inconsistent failover/rollback behavior. Extract into a shared helper (e.g. a common base or free function templated on the impl).

patterns.name: Identical client Init() boilerplate between ObjectClient and KVClient
patterns.rationale: ObjectClient::Init and KVClient::Init are byte-identical (trace guard, InitKvMetrics, impl_->Init(needRollbackState, true), CompleteHandler, return). The same init/rollback sequence is duplicated verbatim; changes to metrics init or rollback handling must be kept in sync manually across clients. Shares the same root cause as the ShutDown duplication and should be factored into a common client lifecycle helper.

patterns.name: Near-identical Set/SetValue pybind lambdas (buffer request + AccessRecorder plumbing)
patterns.rationale: The Set and SetValue lambdas registered in pybind_register_kv.cpp share an almost identical copy-pasted body: trace guard, py::buffer_info request, StringView construction, SetParam init, AccessRecorder(DS_KV_CLIENT_SET), RequestParam population (objectKey/writeMode/ttlSecond) and Record call. Only the exact client.Set overload and the key handling differ. This buffer-to-StringView + access-recording preamble recurs across several .def bindings and should be pulled into a shared helper to avoid drift in the recorded request metadata.
